acceptance_criteria:
1. Returns formatted system message string
2. Includes field descriptions with types
3. Includes marker structure template
4. Includes BAML schema from OutputFormatContent
5. Includes instruction from Signature::instruction()
title: Add typed system message formatting with BAML schema

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content:
Code pointers (as of 2026-01-27):
- Typed path: `crates/dspy-rs/src/predictors/predict.rs` `Predict::call_with_meta` builds ChatAdapter messages, then calls `lm.call` directly; no cache lookup/insert.
- Legacy cache path: `crates/dspy-rs/src/adapter/chat.rs` `ChatAdapter::call` uses `lm.cache` + `cache_handler` with `CacheEntry` (see `crates/dspy-rs/src/utils/cache.rs`).
- Cache key today is `Example` (input fields only); it ignores instruction_override, demos, lm model/params, or signature name.
Suggested insertion points: before `lm.call` in `Predict::call_with_meta` for lookup, and after parse for insert; or move caching to `LM::call` in `crates/dspy-rs/src/core/lm/mod.rs` so both typed and legacy share it.

title: Wire cache into typed predictors (Predict::call). Today caching only exists in ChatAdapter::call using Example as key when lm.cache=true; typed path in crates/dspy-rs/src/predictors/predict.rs calls LM::call directly and bypasses cache. Add cache support for typed calls: either move cache lookup/insert into LM::call, or add a typed cache in Predict::call that builds a stable key including signature name + input values + instruction override + demos + model/params. Ensure cache invalidates on model/temp/max_tokens changes. See crates/dspy-rs/src/adapter/chat.rs for current cache flow and ResponseCache in crates/dspy-rs/src/utils/cache.rs.
